content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
January 6, 1997
Mr. Malcolm Hunter
City Attorney
City of Richmond
City Hall Room 330
2600 Barret Avenue, P.O. Box 4046
Richmond, California 94804
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-96-346
Dear Mr. Hunter:
You have requested advice on the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the “Act”)’ on the behalf of Councilmember Butt as they pertain to an upcoming vote.
QUESTION
When may Councilmember Butt participate in a decision regarding a developer who paid
him in July 1996 for architectural services performed in October 1995 an amount over $250?
CONCLUSION
You have not provided any information regarding the decisions pertaining to the
unnamed developer which may come before the Richmond City Council. Therefore, we can only
provide general advice that Councilmember Butt must consider the unnamed developer a source
of income for 12 months following the receipt ofpayment for the architectural services rendered,
i.e., July 1997. Further, Councilmember Butt may not participate in any decision involving the
unnamed developer until 12 months after the receipt of payment from the unnamed developer,
i.e., July, 1997.
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless
otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections 18000-18954.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No. A-96-346
Page 2
FACTS
Councilmember Butt performed certain architectural services for a developer doing
business in the City ofRichmond. The bill for the services rendered by Councilmember Butt
was sent to the developer in October 1995, but was not paid until July 1996. The amount
received by Councilmember Butt for these services exceeded $250.00
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in, or using his
official position to influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know
he has a financial interest.
An official has a financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material fmancial effect, distinguishable from its effect
on the public generally, on the official or a member of his immediate family, or on:
a)
Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect
investment worth one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.
b)
Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect
interest worth one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.
c)
Any source of income, other than gifts and other than loans by a
commercial lending institution in the regular course of business on terms
available to the public without regard to official status, aggregating two
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided to, received by or
promised to the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.
d)
Any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer,
partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position ofmanagement.
e)
Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts
aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250)2 or more in value provided
to, received by, or promised to the public official within 12 months prior
to the time when the decision is made.
Section 87103.
According to Section 87103(c), Councilmember Butt has a financial interest in a decision
2 The gift limit is adjusted biennially. For 1997, the limit is $290.


File No. A-96-346
Page 3
within the meaning of Section 87100 and is disqualified from participating in decisions involving
the unnamed developer. Assuming there are no other financial ties from the unnamed developer,
Councilmember Butt would be able to participate in a decision involving the unnamed developer
in July 1997, or 12 months after the recezpt ofthe payment from the unnamed developer.
I hope this provides guidance regarding who is a source of income and for what period of
time. If, at a future time, you have a question regarding participation in a specific decision,
please contact this office.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General CounseltL c7tE
By:
Marte Castaflos
Legal Intern, Legal Division
